module.name: orch
module.spec: Init creates an empty .drift/state.xml file by saving an empty State. NOT idempotent: if StateStore.Initialized() reports the project is already initialized (state.xml exists on disk), Init returns ErrAlreadyInitialized without writing. If Initialized() returns a non-not-found error (e.g. corrupt state.xml), Init propagates that error rather than overwriting. See cli.init_idempotent for the user-facing contract. See principles.specs_are_truth.

Todo loads State, scans the filesystem, reconciles specs and markers, builds a CoreAlgorithmContext with TodoAction (propagating baseline Edges), runs the core algorithm to derive closures, and returns the EvaluatedState without saving. The scan's discovered spec-spec Edges are passed via the Scan struct so the core can detect edge add/remove/broken events inside closures. See principles.friction — review without side effects.

ResetClosure acquires an exclusive state lock for the entire Load→modify→Save window, loads State, scans the filesystem, reconciles specs and markers, re-derives closures from the current scan+baseline, and locates the closure with the matching 8-character hash. The core applies each event in the closure to baseline: NODE_CHANGED syncs the baseline hash to scan hash; EDGE_ADDED adds the edge to baseline; EDGE_REMOVED removes it; NODE_REMOVED deletes the node and filters its edges; EDGE_BROKEN is a no-op. Closures containing only broken-edge events are refused with ErrResetClosureOnlyBroken. The orchestrator saves the resulting State (Specs, Markers, Edges), merging scanned spec-spec edges via mergeScannedEdges so newly-cited refs make it into state.xml. ErrResetClosureNotFound is returned when the hash doesn't match any current closure. See principles.friction.

Link acquires an exclusive state lock for the entire Load→modify→Save window, loads State, scans the filesystem, reconciles, validates that the marker and spec exist, rejects duplicate edges, establishes the baseline hash for the linked marker and spec by copying their scan hashes into the reconciled State, and saves the State with a new Edge{From: markerID, To: specID} appended (also merging scanned spec-spec edges into baseline via mergeScannedEdges). Writes baseline files for the spec and marker using their current scanned content. The lock prevents concurrent invocations from silently overwriting each other's edges.

Unlink acquires an exclusive state lock for the entire Load→modify→Save window, loads State, finds the link-style edge matching the given marker and spec, removes it from Edges, and saves the State. If no matching edge exists, returns an error.

ReconcileSpecs compares baselined specs against scanned specs. Baselined specs not found on disk are kept in the result with Deleted=true and their baseline hash preserved (no error). New specs in the scan get Hash="" (no baseline yet) so that DeriveClosures flags them as NODE_CHANGED on the next todo. Existing specs keep their baselined baseline hash but update filepath and line number. See principles.specs_are_truth.

ReconcileMarkers compares baselined markers against scanned markers. Baselined markers not found on disk are kept in the result with Deleted=true and their baseline hash preserved (no error). New markers in the scan get Hash="" (no baseline yet) so that DeriveClosures flags them as NODE_CHANGED on the next todo. Existing markers keep their baselined baseline hash but update filepath and line number. See principles.specs_are_truth.

DiffClosure loads State, scans, reconciles, re-derives closures, and locates the closure by hash. For each node in the closure, it builds a DiffSide (spec or marker) containing: ID, filepath, line range (markers only), baseline hash, current hash, baseline content (from BaselineStore), current content (read from disk), HasBaseline flag, and Deleted flag. Returns ErrDiffClosureNotFound if the hash doesn't match any current closure. Read-only — does not modify state.

DiffAll returns per-closure diff results alongside the evaluated state. For each closure, it computes DiffSides for every node and returns them as a ClosureDiff struct (Hash, Events, Diffs). Used by `drift diff --all` to render every closure's diffs in one pass.
